verses.text: At dawn the women come with spices; the stone is rolled away, and the body gone. Empty tomb. The grave cannot hold the Lord of life.
At dawn the women come with spices; the stone is rolled away, and the body gone. Empty tomb. The grave cannot hold the Lord of life.
At dawn the women come with spices; the stone is rolled away, and the body gone. Empty tomb. The grave cannot hold the Lord of life.
Two in shining garments ask, 'Why seek ye the living among the dead? he is... risen'; remember His words. The great news. The risen Christ fulfils all He foretold.
Two in shining garments ask, 'Why seek ye the living among the dead? he is... risen'; remember His words. The great news. The risen Christ fulfils all He foretold.
Two in shining garments ask, 'Why seek ye the living among the dead? he is... risen'; remember His words. The great news. The risen Christ fulfils all He foretold.
Two in shining garments ask, 'Why seek ye the living among the dead? he is... risen'; remember His words. The great news. The risen Christ fulfils all He foretold.
Two in shining garments ask, 'Why seek ye the living among the dead? he is... risen'; remember His words. The great news. The risen Christ fulfils all He foretold.
They tell the eleven, but their words seem 'idle tales,' and they believe not. Slow to believe. The good news meets unbelieving hearts.
They tell the eleven, but their words seem 'idle tales,' and they believe not. Slow to believe. The good news meets unbelieving hearts.
They tell the eleven, but their words seem 'idle tales,' and they believe not. Slow to believe. The good news meets unbelieving hearts.
Peter runs to the sepulchre, sees the linen, and wonders. Stirred. The empty grave begins to draw him to faith.
Two walk to Emmaus; Jesus draws near, but 'their eyes were holden' that they knew Him not. Hidden Lord. He walks unseen with His grieving own.
Two walk to Emmaus; Jesus draws near, but 'their eyes were holden' that they knew Him not. Hidden Lord. He walks unseen with His grieving own.
Two walk to Emmaus; Jesus draws near, but 'their eyes were holden' that they knew Him not. Hidden Lord. He walks unseen with His grieving own.
Two walk to Emmaus; Jesus draws near, but 'their eyes were holden' that they knew Him not. Hidden Lord. He walks unseen with His grieving own.
What are these sayings? they speak of Jesus, a mighty prophet, condemned and crucified. Dashed hopes. Their sorrow shows they had not understood.
What are these sayings? they speak of Jesus, a mighty prophet, condemned and crucified. Dashed hopes. Their sorrow shows they had not understood.
What are these sayings? they speak of Jesus, a mighty prophet, condemned and crucified. Dashed hopes. Their sorrow shows they had not understood.
What are these sayings? they speak of Jesus, a mighty prophet, condemned and crucified. Dashed hopes. Their sorrow shows they had not understood.
'We trusted... he should have redeemed Israel'; the tomb was found empty. Faltering faith. They hoped, yet could not believe the report.
'We trusted... he should have redeemed Israel'; the tomb was found empty. Faltering faith. They hoped, yet could not believe the report.
'We trusted... he should have redeemed Israel'; the tomb was found empty. Faltering faith. They hoped, yet could not believe the report.
'We trusted... he should have redeemed Israel'; the tomb was found empty. Faltering faith. They hoped, yet could not believe the report.
'O fools, and slow of heart'; ought not Christ to suffer? He expounds the scriptures concerning Himself. Christ in all. The whole Bible points to His suffering and glory.
'O fools, and slow of heart'; ought not Christ to suffer? He expounds the scriptures concerning Himself. Christ in all. The whole Bible points to His suffering and glory.
'O fools, and slow of heart'; ought not Christ to suffer? He expounds the scriptures concerning Himself. Christ in all. The whole Bible points to His suffering and glory.
He makes as to go on; 'Abide with us'; in breaking bread 'their eyes were opened,' and He vanished. Known at the table. Christ reveals Himself in fellowship and grace.
He makes as to go on; 'Abide with us'; in breaking bread 'their eyes were opened,' and He vanished. Known at the table. Christ reveals Himself in fellowship and grace.
He makes as to go on; 'Abide with us'; in breaking bread 'their eyes were opened,' and He vanished. Known at the table. Christ reveals Himself in fellowship and grace.
He makes as to go on; 'Abide with us'; in breaking bread 'their eyes were opened,' and He vanished. Known at the table. Christ reveals Himself in fellowship and grace.
'Did not our heart burn within us?' they return and tell how He was known in breaking of bread. Burning hearts. His word and presence kindle joy and faith.
'Did not our heart burn within us?' they return and tell how He was known in breaking of bread. Burning hearts. His word and presence kindle joy and faith.
'Did not our heart burn within us?' they return and tell how He was known in breaking of bread. Burning hearts. His word and presence kindle joy and faith.
'Did not our heart burn within us?' they return and tell how He was known in breaking of bread. Burning hearts. His word and presence kindle joy and faith.
He stands among them, 'Peace'; 'handle me... a spirit hath not flesh and bones'; He shows His hands and feet. Real risen body. He proves He is no ghost but truly raised.
He stands among them, 'Peace'; 'handle me... a spirit hath not flesh and bones'; He shows His hands and feet. Real risen body. He proves He is no ghost but truly raised.
He stands among them, 'Peace'; 'handle me... a spirit hath not flesh and bones'; He shows His hands and feet. Real risen body. He proves He is no ghost but truly raised.

He stands among them, 'Peace'; 'handle me... a spirit hath not flesh and bones'; He shows His hands and feet. Real risen body. He proves He is no ghost but truly raised.
While they wonder for joy, He asks for meat and eats before them. Proof of life. The risen Lord eats to confirm His resurrection.
While they wonder for joy, He asks for meat and eats before them. Proof of life. The risen Lord eats to confirm His resurrection.
While they wonder for joy, He asks for meat and eats before them. Proof of life. The risen Lord eats to confirm His resurrection.
These are the words I spake; He opens their understanding; 'it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise.' Opened scriptures. He shows His cross and rising were foretold.
These are the words I spake; He opens their understanding; 'it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise.' Opened scriptures. He shows His cross and rising were foretold.
These are the words I spake; He opens their understanding; 'it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise.' Opened scriptures. He shows His cross and rising were foretold.
Repentance and remission shall be preached 'among all nations'; tarry till 'endued with power from on high.' The commission. The gospel goes to the world in the Spirit's power.
Repentance and remission shall be preached 'among all nations'; tarry till 'endued with power from on high.' The commission. The gospel goes to the world in the Spirit's power.
Repentance and remission shall be preached 'among all nations'; tarry till 'endued with power from on high.' The commission. The gospel goes to the world in the Spirit's power.
He leads them to Bethany, blesses them, and is 'carried up into heaven'; they worship and praise God. The ascension. He departs in blessing, and they rejoice.
